output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? A Tenant's Urgent Recovery Guide
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what landlords can deduct, and how to demand your money back fast.
content_outline: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Know Your State Deadline
Can a Landlord Keep Your Security Deposit? Legal Deductions Explained
What Can a Landlord Deduct from Your Security Deposit vs. Normal Wear and Tear
My Landlord Won't Give Me My Deposit Back: Immediate Steps to Take
How to Write a Security Deposit Dispute Letter That Forces Action
What to Do When Your Landlord Doesn't Return Your Deposit: Escalation Guide
key_points: Most states require landlords to return deposits within 14-30 days with an itemized deduction statement
Landlords cannot withhold security deposits for normal wear and tear, only actual damage beyond ordinary use
A formal demand letter is required in many states before tenants can sue for wrongful withholding or double damages
Tenants have strong legal protections, but strict deadlines apply for disputing deductions and filing claims
output.cta: Don't let your landlord keep what's yours. Generate your state-specific security deposit demand letter in minutes and start your recovery today.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlords are withholding, partially deducting, or refusing to return security deposits and need immediate, actionable legal guidance to recover their money
